memory.summary: My plan to finish the assignment analysis first conflicts with cohort_3's lunch invite. Given my short planning horizon, I might just join them instead of doing the reading now.

desires: Not look like I'm ditching the group at the last minute in front of cohort_3
Get out of this room and onto my phone or into a nap before the stress spikes further
Just finish the reading so it's 'done' even if I don't actually understand it yet

goals.description: Go eat lunch with cohort_3 immediately to avoid looking like I'm ditching them, then nap while phone is in hand.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' conflict style makes me terrified of social friction; missing the group chat now would cause immediate awkwardness. My 'impulsive' time horizon means I care more about this instant relief than the long-term benefit of finishing the reading. I'll just mark the tasks as done mentally or quickly later.

goals.description: Do a superficial skim of the reading assignment to tick it off as 'done' without deep understanding.
goals.rationale: My procrastination bias is high and my planning horizon is short. I need to feel productive eventually to satisfy my 'achievement' value, but only if it doesn't cost me sleep or social standing right now. Defer until after the nap.
